items.summaryZh: 一项多方法研究，结合了面对面峰会和德尔菲共识过程，汇集了来自网球管理机构和生态系统的83名参与者，为精英和青少年网球运动员的心理健康护理和促进提供了建议。
items.curatedReasonEn: This consensus study provides recommendations for mental health care and promotion among tennis athletes, including junior and elite players. In sports rehabilitation, you can refer to these cross-organizational expert consensuses and apply them to your service design to more comprehensively support athletes' mental well-being.
items.limitation: 德尔菲共识过程可能受到专家组成员主观判断的影响。
items.limitationEn: The Delphi consensus process might be influenced by the subjective judgments of the expert panel members.

items.title: Prevalence, Patterns, Treatment Outcomes and Costs of Physiotherapy-Managed Musculoskeletal Disorders in Nigeria.
items.summary: A 10-year retrospective study of 1582 patients in Nigeria found that physiotherapy effectively managed musculoskeletal disorders, with detailed data on prevalence, treatment patterns, and costs.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究提供了尼日利亚肌肉骨骼疾病物理治疗的流行病学、模式和成本效益数据。在临床实践中，你可以用这些数据来评估当地需求，并据此优化资源分配和治疗方案。
items.titleZh: 尼日利亚物理治疗管理的肌肉骨骼疾病的患病率、模式、治疗结果和成本
items.summaryZh: 一项对尼日利亚1582名患者进行的长达十年的回顾性研究显示，物理治疗有效管理了肌肉骨骼疾病，并提供了患病率、治疗模式和成本的详细数据。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study provides epidemiological, pattern, and cost-effectiveness data on physiotherapy for musculoskeletal disorders in Nigeria. In clinical practice, you can use this data to assess local needs and optimize resource allocation and treatment protocols accordingly.
items.limitation: 研究为回顾性设计，可能存在数据缺失和选择偏倚。
items.limitationEn: The study's retrospective design may be subject to missing data and selection bias.
items.journal: Physiotherapy research international : the journal for researchers and clinicians in physical therapy

items.title: Building the foundations: organisational leadership for athlete mental health
items.summary: A commentary emphasizes the critical role of organizational leadership in athlete mental health, advocating for a shift from reactive crisis response to proactive prevention and promotion to address the high prevalence of mental health symptoms among elite athletes and support staff.
items.curatedReason: 这项评论强调了在运动员心理健康方面，组织领导力的重要性，应从被动应对危机转向主动预防和促进。在运动康复领域，你需要认识到心理健康是整体健康的重要组成部分，并与团队共同构建支持性的环境，以减少求助障碍。
items.titleZh: 奠定基础：运动员心理健康的组织领导力
items.summaryZh: 一项评论强调了组织领导力在运动员心理健康中的关键作用，主张从被动危机应对转向积极预防和促进，以解决精英运动员和支持人员中普遍存在的心理健康问题。
items.curatedReasonEn: This commentary highlights the importance of organizational leadership in athlete mental health, advocating for a shift from reactive crisis response to proactive prevention and promotion. In sports rehabilitation, you need to recognize mental health as an integral component of overall well-being and collaborate with your team to build a supportive environment that reduces barriers to seeking help.

items.title: An mHealth intervention based on behavior change techniques to promote physical activity and nutrition in older patients with cancer: protocol for an N-of-1 trial
items.summary: This N-of-1 trial protocol outlines an mHealth intervention using an ABA' design to promote physical activity and nutritional behaviors in older patients with cancer, combining ecological momentary assessment and accelerometry to capture temporal dynamics of psychological and behavioral processes.
items.curatedReason: 这是一项 N-of-1 试验方案，并非干预效果的定论。尽管采用了 ABA' 设计，但该研究仍在探索 mHealth 干预对老年癌症患者身体活动和营养行为的潜在影响。在结果公布前，不建议直接采纳其干预模式。
items.titleZh: 基于行为改变技术的移动健康干预以促进老年癌症患者的身体活动和营养：一项N-of-1试验方案
items.summaryZh: 这项N-of-1试验方案概述了一项基于ABA'设计的移动健康干预，旨在促进老年癌症患者的身体活动和营养行为，结合生态瞬时评估和加速度计以捕捉心理和行为过程的时间动态。
items.curatedReasonEn: This is an N-of-1 trial protocol, not a definitive statement on intervention efficacy. While using an ABA' design, this study is still exploring the potential impact of mHealth interventions on physical activity and nutritional behaviors in older cancer patients. Do not adopt its intervention model until results are published.
items.limitation: 这是一项协议研究，尚未公布实际结果。
items.limitationEn: This is a protocol study, and actual results have not yet been published.

items.title: Incomplete Cognitive Assessment in Post-Acute Stroke Rehabilitation: Recovering Clinically Interpretable Relationships from Routine Neuropsychological Data.
items.summary: A retrospective cohort study of 2654 post-acute stroke patients found that incomplete routine neuropsychological data can still yield clinically interpretable relationships among cognitive, clinical, and contextual variables, supporting group-level stratification.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究表明，即使卒中后康复中的认知评估不完整，仍能从中提取有临床意义的关系。在日常实践中，你可以利用现有数据进行认知功能分组和初步评估，同时也要意识到其局限性。
items.titleZh: 急性期后卒中康复中不完整的认知评估：从常规神经心理学数据中恢复临床可解释的关系
items.summaryZh: 一项针对2654名急性期后卒中患者的回顾性队列研究发现，即使是常规神经心理学数据不完整，仍能从中提取出认知、临床和情境变量之间具有临床可解释性的关系，支持了群体分层。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study indicates that even incomplete cognitive assessments in post-stroke rehabilitation can yield clinically interpretable relationships. In daily practice, you can use existing data for cognitive functional grouping and preliminary assessments, while also recognizing their limitations.
items.limitation: 研究为回顾性设计，存在数据不完整性，可能影响结果的代表性。
items.limitationEn: The study has a retrospective design with incomplete data, which may affect the representativeness of the results.

items.title: Figure-of-Eight Taping Combined with Rehabilitation for Multidirectional Shoulder Instability in a School-Age Female Karate Athlete: A Case Report.
items.summary: A case report describes the effective use of figure-of-eight taping combined with rehabilitation to facilitate exercise therapy for multidirectional shoulder instability in a 12-year-old female karate athlete.
items.curatedReason: 这个病例报告展示了八字形贴扎如何辅助治疗多方向肩关节不稳。在复杂病例中，你可以考虑将贴扎作为一种工具来促进患者进行主动运动训练。
items.titleZh: 八字形贴扎联合康复治疗学龄期女性空手道运动员多方向肩关节不稳一例报告
items.summaryZh: 一份病例报告描述了八字形贴扎联合康复训练如何有效促进一名12岁女性空手道运动员多方向肩关节不稳的运动治疗。
items.curatedReasonEn: This case report illustrates how figure-of-eight taping can assist in treating multidirectional shoulder instability. In complex cases, you might consider taping as a tool to facilitate active exercise therapy.
items.limitation: 本研究为单病例报告，证据等级低，结果外推性有限。
items.limitationEn: This is a single case report with a low level of evidence and limited generalizability.
items.journal: International journal of sports physical therapy

items.title: Effects of physical exercise on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis: A systematic review.
items.summary: A systematic review analyzed the effectiveness of different physical exercise interventions on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis, including randomized controlled trials with EDSS scores ≤6.0.
items.curatedReason: 这项系统综述明确了不同物理运动干预对多发性硬化症痉挛的影响。你可以参考综述结论，为 MS 患者的痉挛管理选择更有效的运动策略。
items.titleZh: 体育锻炼对多发性硬化症患者痉挛影响的系统综述
items.summaryZh: 一项系统综述分析了不同体育锻炼干预对多发性硬化症患者痉挛的影响，纳入了 EDSS 评分 ≤6.0 的随机对照试验。
items.curatedReasonEn: This systematic review clarifies the effects of various physical exercise interventions on spasticity in multiple sclerosis. Refer to its conclusions to select more effective exercise strategies for managing spasticity in MS patients.
items.limitation: 由于研究设计异质性，本综述采用叙述性综合分析，未进行荟萃分析。
items.limitationEn: Due to heterogeneity in study designs, this review used narrative synthesis instead of meta-analysis.

items.title: Follow up to the 2018 IOC consensus statement on paediatric ACL injuries: towards a broader vision of care
items.summary: A follow-up to the 2018 IOC consensus statement on pediatric ACL injuries provides an updated vision of care, examining recent advances, current evidence, and persistent challenges in prevention, individualized management, evolving surgical strategies, and long-term holistic care.
items.curatedReason: 这份国际奥委会共识声明的后续报告，拓宽了儿童 ACL 损伤的护理视角。你需要更新对儿童 ACL 损伤预防、个体化管理和长期整体护理的理解，将其纳入你的临床实践考量。
items.titleZh: 2018 年国际奥委会儿童 ACL 损伤共识声明的后续：迈向更广阔的护理视野
items.summaryZh: 2018 年国际奥委会儿童 ACL 损伤共识声明的后续报告，提供了更新的护理愿景，审视了预防、个体化管理、不断发展的手术策略和长期整体护理方面的最新进展、现有证据和持续挑战。
items.curatedReasonEn: This follow-up to the IOC consensus statement broadens the perspective on pediatric ACL injury care. Update your understanding of pediatric ACL injury prevention, individualized management, and long-term holistic care, integrating these into your clinical practice considerations.
